build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: A shop displays purchasable units each round. The player spends gold to buy units and places them on a grid board by dragging. Buying deducts gold; attempting to buy without sufficient gold fails visibly. 0 if there is no shop, if units cannot be purchased, or if gold is not consumed. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Units placed on the board auto-fight during combat phase without player input. Units move toward enemies, attack, use abilities, and die with visible feedback. The combat phase resolves to a winner. 0 if combat requires player input, if units do not fight automatically, or if combat has no visible resolution. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

A gold economy persists across rounds: gold is earned each round (base + interest on savings + win/loss streak bonus), spent on units and rerolls, and the balance carries forward. Interest rewards saving. 0 if gold resets each round, if there is no interest mechanic, or if economy has no strategic depth. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Combining three copies of the same unit upgrades it to a visibly stronger star level (larger sprite, stat boost, or visual effect). The upgrade is permanent and observable. 0 if there is no unit combination system, if upgrades have no visible change, or if copies do not merge. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> draft/position phase -> auto-combat -> damage to loser -> next round -> elimination or victory -> result screen. Player HP decreases when losing a round. Reaching 0 HP eliminates the player. 0 if any phase is missing or if elimination is unreachable. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

At least six distinct unit types exist with different sprites, stats, and combat behaviors (melee vs ranged, tank vs assassin vs support). 0 if fewer than four types exist or if types are visually identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

A synergy system grants bonuses when multiple units of the same class or origin are fielded. Active synergies are shown in a tracker. At least three different synergy types exist with observable effects. 0 if there is no synergy system or if bonuses are invisible. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Multiple AI opponents (at least three) compete simultaneously, each with their own board, HP, and strategy. A scoreboard shows all competitors and their health. 0 if there is only one opponent or if no scoreboard exists. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Board positioning matters: front-row units are targeted first, back-row units are protected. Different formations produce different combat outcomes with the same units. 0 if position has no effect on combat or if all units are placed in a single row. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The shop can be rerolled for gold to see new unit options. Player level increases over time or by spending gold, unlocking higher-tier units in the shop. At least two power tiers of units are observable. 0 if the shop never changes or if no tier system exists. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The HUD shows current gold, player HP, round number, and active synergies in clear non-overlapping regions with icon/gauge treatment. The player can always tell economic and health state. 0 if gold, HP, or round info is missing; plain text/debug rows also score 0. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets.

The board grid has distinct cells, readable ownership, and simultaneous shop, bench, and board zones with clear drop targets. 0 if cells are invisible, sides look identical, regions overlap, or plain grids/text labels carry readability. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets.

Actions produce visible feedback: buying adds to bench, placement moves to board, combat shows attacks/HP, star upgrades transform, and round results show damage. 0 if actions lack observable change, rely on text-only feedback, or jump instantly between states. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets.

Overall art coherence: units, board, shop, UI panels, menus, results, and effects share a fantasy-arena visual language and palette. 0 if styles clash, read as default Godot grey, or rely on mixed placeholders/programmatic shapes.

Unit sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art characters, not colored rectangles. Different unit types are visually distinct with unique silhouettes. Star-level upgrades show visible sprite changes. 0 if units are plain shapes or indistinguishable. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The board uses real textured tiles or illustrated arena flooring. The shop and bench areas have themed backgrounds distinct from the combat board. 0 if the board is a plain colored grid or if all areas share an identical flat background. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets: illustrated shop cards, custom gold/HP icons, synergy badges, scoreboard, and result panels. 0 if UI is unstyled Godot defaults, rectangle/text UI, or programmatic chrome.

Animation and atmosphere: combat attacks, unit deaths, star upgrades, gold changes, and round transitions use animation, tween, or particles. The board has ambient life between rounds. 0 if all events snap instantly with no motion. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
